old_claim.note: Herbal Medicine Preparation supplies the plant-remedy and medicine-making practice explicitly represented in monastic infirmary care; military field medicine is not required by this monastic ward scope.

new_claim_absent: No Abydos herbal-wine dependency remains because medieval monastic recipe collections do not establish descent from the specific Predynastic Egyptian wine preparation.

source_shape.source_locator: The PNAS paper identifies an Abydos herbal-wine residue but does not document medieval monastic manuscripts, wards, pharmacies, or recipe transmission.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source-pinned prerequisite and the monastic healing evidence are separate formulations from distant institutional contexts.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Monastic herbal remedies remain valid node content, but their existence should not be converted into dependence on one unrelated ancient recipe.

ontology_before: All plant-remedy practice was collapsed into one generic prerequisite across unrelated cultures and periods.
ontology_after: Monastic infirmaries retain their own manuscript and institutional evidence without an unsupported cross-cultural recipe lineage.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: monastic_infirmaries no longer directly depends on herbal_medicine_preparation.
would_reject_if: Textual or material evidence directly traced the documented monastic remedies to the scoped Abydos herbal-wine tradition.
short_reason: Monastic remedies do not establish descent from the Abydos herbal-wine process.
